zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当George Joseph Alden于1573年1月27日出生在英国哈普爾素坦普頓时，他的父亲Richard Alden71岁，母亲Eliza Ayres38岁。他去世于1620年9月20日在英国英格兰哈普爾素坦普頓享年47岁，埋葬在英国英格兰哈普爾素坦普頓。

nameMeanings.name: Alden
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from a Middle English personal name. This is either Aldwin, Aldin (representing Old English Ealdwine with loss of -w-) or Middle English Alwin with an intrusive -d- (see Alwin ), or Aldan, a variant of the Anglo-Scandinavian personal name Healfdene (see Haldane ).
Norwegian: habitational name from a farmstead in western Norway, so named because of its situation below a high mountain Alden, from an unattested word ‘high, standing out’.

History: John Alden (c. 1599–1687) was one of the Pilgrim Fathers who sailed on the Mayflower in 1620. He moved from Plymouth to Duxbury, MA, c. 1627. Many of his descendants were merchant seamen, among them James Alden (1810–77), who twice circumnavigated the globe.

variants.name: Alwin
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Alwin or Elwin, a falling together of various Old English personal names: Ælfwine ‘elf friend’, Æthelwine ‘noble friend’, Ealdwine ‘old friend’, and others.
German: from a younger form of the personal name Adalwin (‘noble friend’), see above.

nameMeanings.name: George
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

nameMeanings.name: Joseph
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the biblical Hebrew name Yosef, meaning ‘(God) shall add (another son)’. This was borne by the favourite son of Jacob, whose brothers became jealous of him and sold him into slavery (Genesis 37). He was taken to Egypt, where he rose to become chief steward to Pharaoh, and was eventually reconciled to his brothers when they came to buy corn during a seven-year famine (Genesis 43–7). In the New Testament Joseph is the name of the husband of the Virgin Mary. It is also borne by a rich Jew, Joseph of Arimathea (Matthew 27:57; Mark 15:43; Luke 23:50; John 19:38), who took Jesus down from the Cross, wrapped him in a shroud, and buried him in a rock tomb. According to medieval legend, Joseph of Arimathea brought the Holy Grail to Britain. The name was uncommon in Britain in the Middle Ages but was revived in the mid 16th century and had become popular by the 1630s, remaining so ever since.

variants.name: Josef
meanings: , Dutch, Scandinavian, and Czech spelling of   Joseph  , now also in use in the English‐speaking world.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When George Joseph Alden was born on 27 January 1573, in Southampton, Hampshire, England, his father, Richard Alden, was 71 and his mother, Eliza Ayres, was 38. He died on 20 September 1620, in Southampton, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 47, and was buried in Southampton, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque George Joseph Alden est né le 27 janvier 1573, à Southampton, Hampshire, Angleterre, son père, Richard Alden, avait 71 ans et sa mère, Eliza Ayres, avait 38 ans. Il est décédé le 20 septembre 1620, à Southampton, Hampshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 47 ans, et a été enterré à Southampton, Hampshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als George Joseph Alden am 27. Januar 1573 in Southampton, Hampshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard Alden 71 und seine Mutter Eliza Ayres 38. Er starb am 20. September 1620 im Alter von 47 Jahren in Southampton, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Southampton, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando George Joseph Alden è nato il il 27 gennaio 1573 nel Southampton, Hampshire, England, il suo padre, Richard Alden, aveva 71 anni e il suo madre, Eliza Ayres, aveva 38 anni. Morì il 20 settembre 1620, nel Southampton, Hampshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 47 anni, e fu sepolto a Southampton, Hampshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.
